data.title: Tokai Corp. Lifts Full-Year Earnings Forecast on Strong Rental and Healthcare Demand
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tokai ( (JP:9729) ) has issued an update.

Tokai Corp. has raised its consolidated earnings forecast for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, citing stronger-than-expected performance across several core operations. The company now expects net sales of ¥159.6 billion, up 1.1% from its previous forecast, with operating profit projected to rise 13.6% and ordinary profit 16.6%, while profit attributable to owners of parent is seen 10.2% higher than earlier guidance.

Management attributes the upgrade to steady growth in its core rental business, higher prescription unit prices at dispensing pharmacies driven by increased use of high-cost drugs, and robust bedding and linen supply demand on the back of strong hotel occupancy and price revisions. Profitability is further supported by cost reductions from more efficient management of rental materials in the elderly care equipment business, partially offset by expected impairment losses on land at the Saitama Plant and certain pharmacy stores, suggesting resilient underlying operations despite one-off charges.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9729) stock is a Buy with a Yen2797.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tokai stock, see the JP:9729 Stock Forecast page.

More about Tokai

Tokai Corp., listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, operates a diversified service portfolio centered on a core rental business, dispensing pharmacy operations, and bedding and linen supply services. The company also runs an elderly care equipment rental segment and serves institutional clients such as hotels and care facilities, making it sensitive to healthcare demand, occupancy trends, and service pricing dynamics in Japan.

YTD Price Performance: 2.12%

Average Trading Volume: 41,061

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen81.87B

data.title: Karnov deepens share buyback to tighten capital structure and boost shareholder value
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Karnov Group AB ( (SE:KAR) ) has shared an announcement.

Karnov Group AB, a Stockholm-listed provider of legal knowledge and workflow solutions, delivers digital tools and expert content to more than 400,000 legal professionals across several European markets. Backed by over 7,000 authors and a workforce of about 1,100 employees, the company has established itself as a critical information and decision-support partner for the legal sector in the Nordics and Southern Europe.

The company has repurchased 2,360,065 of its own series A shares between 30 March and 2 April 2026 under an ongoing buyback programme aimed at optimising its capital structure and enhancing shareholder value. Executed via DNB Carnegie Investment Bank on Nasdaq Stockholm, the transactions lift Karnov’s treasury holding to 9,934,168 shares out of a total 108,102,047, underscoring management’s continued use of share repurchases as a financial tool to adjust leverage and signal confidence in the business.

By reducing the free float of its series A shares, the buyback may provide earnings-per-share support and increase capital efficiency, which could benefit existing investors if future performance meets expectations. The move also reinforces Karnov’s ability to deploy balance-sheet flexibility in pursuit of shareholder returns while maintaining its strategic focus on legal information services across its core European markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:KAR) stock is a Buy with a SEK115.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Karnov Group AB stock, see the SE:KAR Stock Forecast page.

More about Karnov Group AB

Karnov Group AB is a European provider of mission-critical knowledge and workflow solutions for legal professionals, offering curated legal content and tools to support faster, better decision-making. The company serves more than 400,000 users through contributions from over 7,000 authors and experts, and operates from offices in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, France, Spain and Portugal, with its shares listed on Nasdaq Stockholm’s Mid Cap segment under the ticker KAR.

Its platform underpins legal work across multiple jurisdictions, positioning Karnov as a key infrastructure player in the European legal information market. With around 1,100 employees, the group focuses on digital content and workflow services that help clear the path to justice for a broad base of professional users.

data.description:

Tosei ( (JP:8923) ) has shared an update.

Tosei Corporation reported strong first-quarter results for the period ended February 28, 2026, with revenue rising 31.3% year on year to ¥60.5 billion and operating profit up 25.8% to ¥15.5 billion. Profit attributable to owners of the parent climbed 24.5% to ¥10.2 billion, and basic earnings per share, adjusted for a recent 2-for-1 share split, increased to ¥104.99.

The company’s financial position improved as equity attributable to owners of the parent increased to ¥108.3 billion, lifting the equity ratio to 36.0% despite a slight decline in total assets. For the full fiscal year ending November 30, 2026, Tosei is maintaining its forecast of nearly 30% revenue growth and modest profit expansion, while planning a total annual dividend equivalent to ¥55 per share post-split, underscoring confidence in earnings and shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:8923) stock is a Buy with a Yen1687.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tosei stock, see the JP:8923 Stock Forecast page.

More about Tosei

Tosei Corporation is a Japan-based real estate company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and Singapore Exchange. It operates under IFRS and focuses on property-related businesses, generating revenue and profit mainly from real estate investment and development in its core domestic markets.

Japanese jeweler Vérité Co., Ltd. reported strong March 2026 trading, with total sales from all stores rising sharply year on year, driven by higher average customer spending and increased footfall. data.title: Indian Energy Exchange Posts Record Power Volumes and Lower Prices in FY26
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Indian Energy Exchange Ltd. ( (IN:IEX) ).

Indian Energy Exchange reported record operational performance in fiscal 2026, with its highest ever annual traded electricity volume of 141 billion units, a 17% year-on-year rise, and record yearly trading of 18.72 million Renewable Energy Certificates, up 5%. This growth was supported by a 41% jump in Real-Time Market volumes, a 23% increase in the Green Market, and significantly lower average prices in the Day-Ahead and Real-Time segments, reflecting improved supply liquidity from stronger renewable and coal-based generation.

In Q4 FY26, IEX achieved its highest quarterly traded electricity volume of 39.4 billion units, up 24.3% year-on-year, while Real-Time Market and Green Market volumes grew 48.2% and 26.5% respectively. March 2026 also marked a record monthly volume of 13.9 billion units, with strong growth in real-time and green segments, underscoring IEX’s strengthening position as a key platform for efficiently meeting India’s modestly rising power demand at lower clearing prices.

More about Indian Energy Exchange Ltd.

Indian Energy Exchange Ltd. is India’s premier electricity trading platform, facilitating spot power transactions across products like the Day-Ahead Market, Real-Time Market, Green Market and Renewable Energy Certificates. It serves distribution companies, generators, and industrial consumers, helping balance national power demand and supply through competitive market-based price discovery.

The latest update is out from Arealink Co ( (JP:8914) ).

Arealink Co., Ltd. reported that its Hello Storage network (excluding partner facilities) reached 119,016 total units at the end of March 2026, with 96,306 units in use and an overall utilization rate of 80.92%. Utilization at existing facilities remained strong at 87.13%, while newly opened facilities posted a lower 37.18% utilization as capacity expansion continued.

Management noted that the large number of new openings is weighing on the overall utilization rate, but the stable performance of existing sites underscores resilient underlying demand. In March 2026 alone, the company added 24 new facilities with 978 units and further expanded through partner-system openings, indicating an aggressive growth strategy that could enhance future revenue potential once newly added capacity matures.

More about Arealink Co

Arealink Co., Ltd. operates in the self-storage and container storage industry in Japan under the Hello Storage brand. The company develops and manages container-based and mini self-storage facilities across multiple prefectures, targeting urban and suburban customers who need additional storage space for personal and business use.

data.title: IK HOLDINGS’ March Sales Slip as Direct Marketing Weakness Drags Growth
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from IK HOLDINGS Co.,Ltd. ( (JP:2722) ).

IK HOLDINGS Co.,Ltd. reported consolidated March 2026 sales of ¥1,127 million, down 2.3% year on year, with cumulative sales for the current fiscal year to March at ¥12,358 million, representing 95.4% of the prior-year level. The figures underscore a deceleration versus last year’s strong growth phase, as the company works through a period of softer momentum.

By segment, March sales in the direct marketing business fell to ¥201 million, just 78.8% of the prior-year month, extending a prolonged slump in that operation. In contrast, the sales marketing business delivered ¥926 million in March sales, up 3.0% year on year, highlighting that the group’s performance increasingly depends on this segment as direct marketing remains a drag on overall growth.

The monthly data also show that, after double-digit year-on-year gains through most of FY May 2025, IK HOLDINGS has shifted into a more subdued trajectory since late 2025. This moderation in top-line expansion, combined with persistent weakness in direct marketing, suggests a more challenging operating environment and a greater need to reinforce growth drivers in the core sales marketing arm to sustain its market position.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:2722) stock is a Hold with a Yen428.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IK HOLDINGS Co.,Ltd. stock, see the JP:2722 Stock Forecast page.

More about IK HOLDINGS Co.,Ltd.

IK HOLDINGS Co.,Ltd. is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Standard Market and the Nagoya Stock Exchange Premier Market, operating mainly in direct marketing and sales marketing businesses. The group generates revenue through a direct marketing segment, which has recently been under pressure, and a larger sales marketing segment that has been showing comparatively steadier growth.

Taiheiyo Cement ( (JP:5233) ) has provided an update.

Taiheiyo Cement has issued a notice correcting materials related to its review of the 26 Medium-Term Management Plan and future direction, originally released on March 30, 2026. The company clarified that references to its decarbonization roadmap should read Carbon Neutrality Strategy 2050, not 2025, and has reissued the revised documents to ensure accurate disclosure on its long-term environmental commitments.

The correction indicates that Taiheiyo’s carbon-neutral goal is positioned as a 2050 target, aligning with broader industry and policy timelines rather than an immediate mid-decade deadline. This adjustment may influence how investors and stakeholders evaluate the feasibility and pacing of Taiheiyo’s sustainability strategy in relation to its capital allocation and valuation objectives, including its stated aim of achieving a price-to-book ratio above 1x.

Taiheiyo Cement is a Japanese cement manufacturer operating in the building materials and construction sector. The company focuses on producing cement and related materials while advancing strategies such as carbon neutrality to align its operations with long-term environmental and market demands.

data.title: Emerald Expositions Earnings Call Highlights Growth Path
data.description:
Emerald Expositions Events ((EEX)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Emerald Expositions Events’ latest earnings call struck an upbeat tone around growth, profitability and balance sheet strength, even as management acknowledged some messy near-term optics. Revenue and adjusted EBITDA rose sharply for 2025, acquisitions are reshaping the portfolio, and 2026 guidance was framed as both achievable and cash generative despite higher SG&A and a still-soft content unit.

Robust Top-Line Growth Across 2025

Emerald posted full-year 2025 revenue of $463.4 million, up 16.2% year over year, with Q4 revenue climbing to $132.7 million from $106.8 million. Reported organic growth was modest at 1.1% for the year and 0.3% in Q4, but pro forma organic growth assuming 2025 deals had been owned in 2024 was a healthier 4.8% for the year and 5.3% in Q4.

Margin Expansion Drives Adjusted EBITDA Gains

Adjusted EBITDA jumped 25% to $127.1 million in 2025, supported by both higher revenue and improved operations across core shows. In Q4, adjusted EBITDA reached $36.3 million, up 9.7% year over year, demonstrating that the business is scaling profitably even as it absorbs newly acquired brands.

Strategic M&A Repositions the Events Portfolio

Management highlighted recent acquisitions such as This is Beyond, Insurtech Insights and Generis as key to expanding into luxury, manufacturing and executive peer networks. At the same time, Emerald exited underperforming brands, aiming to build a more diversified, higher-quality events platform designed for durable, recurring growth.

Balance Sheet Flexibility Underpins Strategy

Emerald closed 2025 with $100.9 million of cash and total liquidity of $210.4 million, including a $110 million revolver. Net debt to covenant EBITDA stood at 2.86 times, below the firm’s sub-3.0 times target, giving management room to pursue disciplined M&A and shareholder returns.

Capital Returns Continue via Buybacks and Dividends

The company repurchased 4,058,604 shares in 2025 at an average price of $4.32, including 282,386 shares in Q4 at $4.56. With $24.6 million still available under its authorization and a quarterly dividend of $0.015 per share, Emerald signaled ongoing commitment to returning capital alongside growth investments.

High Contracted Revenue Supports Sales Visibility

Management reported that over 70% of expected 2026 revenue is already contracted, reflecting strong rebooking trends across major shows. This high level of forward sales provides unusual visibility into near-term revenue and underpins confidence in the company’s 2026 outlook.

AI and Automation Modernize Operations

Emerald is rolling out AI agents at events to answer exhibitor questions and cut inbound support volume, with early deployments focused on improving customer experience. Additional pilots in finance, marketing and customer service are aimed at boosting automation, scalability and, over time, margin expansion.

Venue Disruptions Ease as Las Vegas Work Ends

Construction at the Las Vegas Convention Center wrapped up at the end of 2025, removing a notable operational headwind for several key shows. Management expects 2026 events to benefit from both reduced disruption and the appeal of a fully renovated venue.

SG&A Spike Skews Cost Picture

SG&A expenses surged to $88.7 million in Q4 from $34.6 million a year earlier and to $241.2 million for the full year from $170.4 million. The increase was mainly driven by remeasurement of contingent consideration on acquisitions, plus transaction and integration costs, masking underlying operating leverage.

Free Cash Flow Hit by Deal Timing and Fees

Free cash flow slipped to $10.1 million in Q4 from $18.4 million and to $34.3 million for 2025 from $37.0 million. Management stressed that acquisition timing and refinancing costs created a $36.6 million headwind, suggesting underlying cash generation is stronger than the reported figures imply.

Growth Leaning on Acquisitions Raises Execution Risk

A significant portion of Emerald’s recent growth has come from 2025 acquisitions, with pro forma organic metrics notably above reported organic numbers. While management is confident in integration, contingent consideration and related costs are inflating expenses and highlight near-term execution risk.

Content Segment Remains a Drag

The Content and Commerce business remains a small, single-digit share of revenue, with the content arm underperforming amid post-pandemic shifts and digital advertising pressure. New lead-generation offerings launched in late 2025 have attracted interest but are not expected to be a major growth driver in 2026.

Strategic Review and Macro Risks Add Uncertainty

Emerald flagged moderate one-time costs tied to an ongoing board-led strategic review, which could rise in the first half of 2026 and weigh on near-term earnings. The company also remains heavily U.S.-centric, leaving results sensitive to domestic macro trends, tariffs and broader travel or geopolitical concerns.

Guidance Signals Earnings and Cash Flow Momentum

For 2026, Emerald guided to revenue of $490.0–$495.0 million and adjusted EBITDA of $137.5–$142.5 million, implying roughly 6% revenue and 10% EBITDA growth at the midpoint. Management expects free cash flow of about $85–$90 million, pointing to strong conversion from earnings to cash, supported by a solid balance sheet and ongoing capital returns.

Emerald’s earnings call painted a picture of a business leaning into acquisitions and modernization to drive profitable growth, while managing through accounting noise and weaker legacy content assets. With high revenue visibility, improving venues and rising free cash flow expected in 2026, investors are likely to focus on execution against integration and cost-control milestones in the year ahead.
